data.title: Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Notches Up With Pilot Partnership
data.description:
While electric vehicle giant Tesla's (TSLA) status as an electric vehicle giant is under fire these days, there are some signs its move to be part of the electric semi race is taking shape. Tesla recently launched a partnership effort with Pilot Travel Centers to bring charging stations to the truck stops. The move proved welcome for investors, who sent shares up fractionally in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla will bring its line of Semi Chargers to several Pilot stations, some of which will be active as early as this summer. Most of the stops will be along I-5, I-10, and "several major corridors where the need for heavy-duty charging is highest." The Pilot stations that have Tesla Semi Chargers will have them in blocks of four to eight, reports note, and use the V4 cabinet charging technology to deliver power faster.

Pilot's senior vice president of alternative fuels, Shannon Sturgil, noted, "Helping to shape the future of energy is a strategic pillar in meeting the needs of our guests and the North American transportation industry. Heavy-duty charging is yet another extension of our exploration into alternative fuel offerings, and we're happy to partner with a leader in the space that provides turnkey solutions and deploys them quickly."

Puzzling Name Change May Be Ahead

Tesla is also planning a move that might puzzle some car buyers, as it sets out to change the name of the Model Y, but only the base model of the Model Y. Thankfully, the name change here should prove helpful to most, as Tesla renames the Model Y Standard to the Model Y Rear-Wheel Drive. Oddly, the other model in the Model Y series, the Model Y Premium, also comes with rear-wheel drive, so the distinction might be a bit puzzling.

Tesla, meanwhile, has not confirmed the name change, so this may never actually happen. Those interested in buying a Tesla will need to check the website, or hit a dealership, to find out if the name change has gone through. Still, why Tesla suddenly feels the need to highlight a feature on the Standard so heavily is, at best, unclear.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 10 Buys, eight Holds, and six Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 10.74% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $406.87 per share implies 6.05% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: 3 Best Dividend Aristocrat Stocks to Buy Now, 01/28/2026
data.description:
If you're aiming to assemble a reliable income portfolio in 2026, dividend aristocrat stocks can be an attractive route to locking in a steady flow of cash distributions. These three stocks are top picks: General Dynamics (GD), Cincinnati Financial (CINF), and A. O. Smith Corporation (AOS).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Which Dividend Aristocrat Stocks Are the Best to Buy?

According to Wall Street analysts, the three stocks listed below are worth buying. Each stock has a dividend payout ratio of at least 45%, meaning that each company distributes at 45% of its net earnings to shareholders as dividends. Furthermore, each company currently has a Buy rating based on consensus from top analysts or broader Wall Street agreement, with significant upside potential.

Be sure to click on any ticker to thoroughly research the stock before you decide whether to add it to your portfolio. Here are today's top dividend aristocrat stock picks from TipRanks.

  1. General Dynamics (GD) — General Dynamics is an American aerospace and defense company. The Virginia-based company's payout ratio is currently 78.77%. General Dynamics paid $1.50 per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 1.65%. GD stock currently holds a Moderate Buy consensus rating from five Wall Street analysts, with an average price target of $398.50, implying 11.48% upside potential.
  2. Cincinnati Financial (CINF) — Cincinnati Financial is an American company that specializes in providing property casualty insurance products. The Ohio-based company's dividend payout ratio currently stands at 45.08%. Cincinnati paid 87 cents per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 2.20%. CINF stock currently holds a Moderate Buy consensus from five analysts on Wall Street, with an average price target of $175 that suggests 10.46% upside.
  3. A. O. Smith Corporation (AOS)  — This is an American water solutions company that manufactures residential and commercial water heaters, boilers, tanks, and water treatment products. The Wisconsin-based company currently has a dividend payout ratio of 72.44%. It paid 36 cents per share in its last dividend issuance, with a dividend yield of 1.94%. AOS stock currently holds a Moderate Buy consensus rating from seven Wall Street analysts, with an average price target of $77.17, implying approximately 10% upside potential.

What Are Dividend Aristocrats?

Dividend aristocrats are a small cadre of companies within the S&P 500 (SPX) that have managed to not only issue a dividend every single year, but also ratchet those payments higher annually for at least a quarter of a century. That kind of long-term dividend discipline tends to appeal to investors who care more about a stable, growing income stream than about trying to time sharp, short-lived moves in share prices.

On top of that, newly created spinoff companies can qualify for dividend aristocrat status as well, since the market generally assumes they inherit their former parent's established habit of raising dividends year after year.

Interested in More Dividend Aristocrat Stocks?

The page provides an exhaustive list of all dividend aristocrats, including their most recent dividend yields, previous dividend amounts, payout ratios, and other details.

data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Last-Minute Call: Wall Street Weighs In on Tesla (TSLA) Ahead of Tonight's Earnings
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) heads into its fourth-quarter earnings report tonight, with Wall Street making its final calls before the closing bell. One key piece of the puzzle is already in – fourth-quarter delivery figures – and they didn't exactly light up the scoreboard.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Tesla missed consensus expectations for both Q4 and the full year, delivering 418,227 vehicles, below the Street's forecast of 422,850 in Q4, while for FY25, total deliveries reached 1,636,129 vehicles, below the prognosticators' 1,640,752 target. Sales in the quarter were largely impacted by the expiration of the EV tax credit in September and, ultimately, FY25 represented the company's second straight year of declining auto sales.

With Tesla likely to outline its 2026 vehicle delivery outlook on the earnings call, investors will obviously be keen to find out whether it guides to growth in its auto business or signals another year of lower sales.

Numbers-wise, consensus is calling for 4Q25 revenue of approximately $24.5 billion, GAAP gross margin of around 17.0%, and non-GAAP diluted EPS of $0.43.

Investors will also be watching for updates from Elon Musk on Tesla's robotaxi ambitions. With the safety driver recently removed from testing in Austin, Musk is expected to share more color on expansion plans into multiple metro areas.

On the software side, Tesla continues to push its Full Self-Driving ambitions. After introducing FSD (Supervised) in Australia and New Zealand in Q3, the company is laying the groundwork for a broader rollout in China and a first launch in Europe, pending regulatory approval. Together, these updates could shape how investors view Tesla's growth narrative beyond cars alone.

Cantor analyst Andres Sheppard thinks the forthcoming commercialization of FSD in China and Europe are "material catalysts" with margin upside.

Separately, at the World Economic Forum, Musk indicated that Tesla's humanoid robot, Optimus, is expected to be available for consumer purchase beginning in 2H27. MarketsandMarkets also recently provided its outlook for the humanoid robotics market, estimating global growth from approximately $2.9 billion in 2025 to $15.3 billion by 2030, implying a 39.2% CAGR.

"We believe this is a material opportunity for TSLA over the long term, and on the call, we hope for further details around Optimus' mass production timeline, fleet size, or ASPs," Sheppard noted.

Sheppard is also looking for progress on another autonomous angle – trucking. The analyst continues to believe Tesla will enter the self-driving truck market this decade and expects an update on the timing of Semi production, for which he sees SOP (start of production) this year.

Ahead of the print, Sheppard assigns Tesla shares an Overweight (i.e., Buy) rating, backed by a $510 price target. Should the figure be met, investors will be pocketing returns of 17% a year from now. (To watch Sheppard's track record, click here)

9 other analysts are in the bullish camp, but they're balanced out by 8 Holds and 7 Sells, leaving Tesla with a lukewarm Hold (i.e., Neutral) consensus rating overall. Meanwhile, the Street's average price target of $398.38 suggests the stock could slip about 8% over the next 12 months. (See TSLA stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Flat ahead of Fed Rate Decision as Trump Ramps Pressure on Iran
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is holding steady on Wednesday ahead of the first Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting of the year at 2 p.m. Eastern Time today. The central bank is widely expected to maintain interest rates, with CME's FedWatch tool pricing in 97.2% odds of that outcome.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ahead of the meeting, President Trump urged Iran to strike a deal with the U.S. to halt its nuclear development. "A massive Armada is heading to Iran," Trump said in a Truth Social post. "It is moving quickly, with great power, enthusiasm, and purpose." In response, Iran's UN Mission in New York said the country is open to dialogue but is also ready to defend itself "like never before."

Widespread protests across Iran have recaptured the attention of the U.S. Iranian-focused human rights group HRANA estimates that over 6,000 people have lost their lives during the unrest, which was spurred by a sharp rise in the cost of living and long-standing political grievances.

Elsewhere, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) and Bank of America (BAC) both announced that they would match the Trump account contribution of $1,000 for their U.S. employees. Trump accounts, which were passed into law as part of the One Big Beautiful Bill, will provide American children born between January 2025 and December 2028 with a one-time contribution of $1,000. Withdrawals are locked until the child reaches the age of 18, and individuals and employers can contribute up to $5,000 per year. The two banks join several other companies that have pledged contribution matches, including BlackRock (BLK), Charles Schwab (SCHW), and Robinhood (HOOD).

The Dow Jones is down by 0.02% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: "Current Threat Landscape" Opens Corporate Jet to Niccol, Starbucks Stock (NASDAQ:SBUX) Jumps Regardless
data.description:
Shareholders of coffee giant Starbucks (SBUX) should brace themselves to pay a lot more for the company's jet fuel, because Brian Niccol just got full access to the corporate jet. A recent security review apparently decided life was not sufficiently safe for Niccol, and thus, being able to fly is a vital business function. Shareholders were oddly happy about this development as well, and sent shares up over 2% in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Previously, Niccol's use of the corporate jet was limited to the equivalent of however much $250,000 worth of service would buy. But considering a combination of "enhanced media attention," "the current threat landscape," and "the existence of credible threat actors," Starbucks' board basically threw out the guardrails to let Niccol fly wherever he pleased.

Even the capped use came under fire from environmental watchdog groups who called the move an example of "…environmental hypocrisy and leadership double standards." It turns out protecting Niccol costs Starbucks fairly big money; total costs for security were $1.1 million, and the jet alone cost just under $1 million. Starbucks shelled out over $370,000 for temporary housing for Niccol as well.

More Protein

Starbucks' plan to put more protein in its customers' diets is moving along. Niccol also noted that Starbucks was set to focus more on "wellness items," which included things like personalized energy drinks and snacks with more protein and fiber contained therein. This is to be particularly true in the afternoon, reports note, which is when people are starting to crash from lunch and the end of the workday anyway.

Reports noted that the protein cold foam and enriched drink options that Starbucks brought out in 2025 were already something of a hit. Those moves were given credit for breaking the company's seven-quarter sales decline, which was likely welcome news to shareholders.

Is Starbucks Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on SBUX stock based on 13 Buys, eight Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After an 11.84% loss in its share price over the past year, the average SBUX price target of $98.32 per share implies 0.66% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Gold Outpacing the S&P 500 is Bad News for Stocks, Warns Stifel
data.description:
A divergence between gold (XAUUSD) and the S&P 500 (SPX) could spell trouble for stocks, according to Stifel Chief Equity Strategist Barry Bannister. During the past year, the precious metal has surged 90%, while the benchmark index has gained 15%. Similar episodes of gold outperforming stocks significantly have only occurred four times during the past 100 years, and the subsequent returns for stocks weren't encouraging.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"The S&P 500 was range-bound for years after the S&P 500 relative to gold crossed this threshold in the past," wrote Bannister.

Gold Gains on Dollar Decline and Global Risks

Bannister added that gold's outperformance may reflect an exit from fiat currency. That comes amid a weakening dollar, with the U.S. Dollar Index falling by 10.5% over the past year. The index tracks the dollar's performance against a basket of major global currencies. Gold tends to benefit from a weaker dollar, as it becomes cheaper for foreign buyers.

Gold has also received boosts from rising geopolitical tensions, tariffs, persistent inflation, and risks to the Fed's independence.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Google to Pay $135M to Settle Lawsuit from Smartphone Users
data.description:
Google (GOOGL) has agreed to pay $135 million to settle a class-action lawsuit from smartphone users who said that the tech giant collected their cellular data without permission. The proposed settlement was filed on Tuesday in federal court in San Jose, California, and still needs a judge's approval. The case covers people who have used Android phones since November 12, 2017. Although Google agreed to the settlement, the company denied any wrongdoing, with spokesperson Jose Castaneda saying it resolved the case to move forward and provide clearer information about how its services work.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to the lawsuit, users alleged that Google continued to collect cellular data even after they closed Google apps, turned off location sharing, or locked their phone screens. They argued that this data, which users paid for through their mobile carriers, was used to support Google's product development and targeted advertising. The plaintiffs described this practice as "conversion," a legal term for wrongly taking and using someone else's property. As part of the settlement, Google will change how Android handles data sharing.

Indeed, the company will no longer transfer cellular data without getting user consent during phone setup, will make it easier to stop these transfers using simple settings, and will clearly explain the practice in Google Play's terms of service. Impressively, a lawyer for the users said that the $135 million settlement is the largest ever in a conversion case, though individual payments are capped at $100 per person. Still, the plaintiffs' lawyers may seek up to $39.8 million from the settlement to cover legal fees.

Is GOOGL Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on GOOGL stock based on 26 Buys and seven Holds assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average GOOGL price target of $347.70 per share implies 4.4% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: $117.5 Million Settlement Managest to Improve Comcast Stock (NASDAQ:CMCSA)
data.description:
Normally, the news that a company is on the hook for a nine-figure settlement would be considered bad news. But for entertainment giant Comcast (CMCSA), it turns out having to pay $117.5 million is considered reasonably good news. The response from shareholders made that much oddly clear as shares notched up fractionally in Wednesday morning's trading.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The $117.5 million settlement will go to settle a class action lawsuit stemming from the CitrixBleed incident from back in 2023. The CitrixBleed incident allowed hackers to take over legitimate user sessions on NetScaler ADC, as well as Gateway appliances, to steal ai-in-advertising">user credentials. Comcast found out about the incident in October 2023, disclosed it in December 2023, and then went to court over it, leading to the class action settlement announced.

Comcast will be reimbursing over 31 million people in this lawsuit, reports note, and there will be different kinds of settlements available to those involved. Those who have proof of out-of-pocket losses will be able to submit their proof for up to $10,000 of reimbursement. Alternatively, they can also file for a "Lost Time" settlement for hours spent fixing the fallout. But with 31 million people involved and a total pool of $117.5 million to disburse, the actual payouts may not go over well.

Express Pass Changes

Those considering a trip to Florida this year—not soon enough for some, who are still feeling the impact of recent cold—will have something to consider as Universal Orlando alters its Express Pass system. Express Passes are nothing new. These are those little slips that allow users to skip lines, allowing instead access to a shorter line.

Formerly, customers could buy discounted Express Pass options after 4:00 p.m. The idea was the day was coming to an end anyway, so why not make a little extra in sales cutting down people's wait times for the last few hours of the day? Now, that option has quietly moved online. Availability is uncertain at best, but for those hoping to snag such a pass the day of, that option is now likely out of the picture.

Is Comcast Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on CMCSA stock based on seven Buys, 12 Holds and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 23.18% loss in its share price over the past year, the average CMCSA price target of $33.18 per share implies 14.61% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Polymarket Odds: Will AAPL, V, MA Beat Earnings Tomorrow?
data.description:
It's been a busy week for earnings, although several reports remain on deck. On Thursday, Apple (AAPL), Visa (V), and Mastercard (MA) will report their results for the period ended December 31, 2025. Traders are already weighing in on their expectations through Polymarket.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

  • Apple is expected to report an EPS of $2.67
  • Visa is expected to report an adjusted EPS of $3.14
  • Mastercard is expected to report an adjusted EPS of $4.24

What is Polymarket?

Polymarket is a prediction platform where traders bet on the outcomes of real-world events, including earnings reports, elections, and economic indicators. Polymarket odds reflect expectations and can provide insight into investor sentiment.

Will Apple Beat Earnings?

Apple has beaten earnings during 17 of the last 18 quarters, with the only miss coming in Q1 of 2023. Polymarket traders are confident that the tech giant will top its estimated EPS of $2.67, giving it a 94% chance.

Will Visa Beat Earnings?

Like Apple, Visa has a solid track record of beating earnings with a 100% success rate during the past 18 quarters. Polymarket traders assign 88% odds that the payment processing company will top its estimated adjusted EPS of $3.14.

Will Mastercard Beat Earnings?

Mastercard has also beaten earnings in each of the past 18 quarters. Polymarket gives the company a 91% chance of surpassing its estimated adjusted EPS of $4.24.

It's worth pointing out that Polymarket odds reflect expectations, not guaranteed outcomes. The odds represent the views and expectations of traders, but actual earnings results can differ significantly from these predictions. Investors should treat Polymarket data as just one tool when evaluating their investment decisions.
